This invalidates the stated inductive guarantee and must be flagged.",
    "R1.3": "The single quantitative claim (6.3% selective-accuracy gain over GraphSAGE-Abstain at 80% coverage on OGB-Arxiv) is asserted without a reference to a table, figure, or appendix; checking whether the attributed cause (abstention head + inductive design) is actually separable from confounds is within scope of 'check this' on a technical artifact."
  },

rationale.R1.1: The artifact makes a specific contribution claim (selective node classification via learned abstention) and a design-goal claim (full inductiveness); verifying the thesis is coherent and internally sound is expected under any reasonable reading of 'check this'.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the artifact contains a direct logical contradiction. It asserts the model is 'fully inductive' yet introduces a per-node learned embedding e_v for each node v in the input graph — embeddings that cannot exist for nodes unseen at training time. This invalidates the stated inductive guarantee and must be flagged.
rationale.R1.3: The single quantitative claim (6.3% selective-accuracy gain over GraphSAGE-Abstain at 80% coverage on OGB-Arxiv) is asserted without a reference to a table, figure, or appendix; checking whether the attributed cause (abstention head + inductive design) is actually separable from confounds is within scope of 'check this' on a technical artifact.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this check cover writing quality and narrative presentation (R1.5, R1.6) in addition to technical and logical correctness — or is catching errors in reasoning and evidence alignment the primary goal?
